findings.description: Probabilistic mitigation over an unchanged skip-on-null-identity policy. Retry exhaustion still returns skipped-unverified; core 'no identity -> no kill -> leak' path intact.

Immediate-delete race uncovered by retry: session deleted before capture completes still leaks regardless of retry count (not a new regression, but retry provides zero benefit for this exact reported scenario).
Retry amplifies CIM/powershell load precisely when system is degraded: up to 3x heavy probe spawns per session, ~9.6s worst-case capture wall-time; 200/400ms backoff negligible vs 3s timeout.
Already-exited process returns null instantly and still increments identityCaptureFailed alongside genuine timeouts -- counter conflates benign and broken cases, muddying alerting semantics.

In-flight probe subprocess not cancellable; raised timeout (3s vs 1s) lengthens orphaned-probe lifetime after session finalization (guards prevent stale-data access, but subprocess itself lingers longer).
Regression tests coupled to real wall-clock timers (await delay(900)) with exact-count assertions -- timing drift risk under load/CI.
Scope leans large for hot-fix: new strict() config schema field + 3 new WS telemetry protocol fields -- backward-compatible but should be captured by mandated kiwi-srs-sync.

root_limitation_note: 재시도/타임아웃 상향은 leak 확률을 낮추지만 '신원 미확보시 kill 스킵' 정책 자체는 그대로 남아 재시도 소진·즉시삭제 경로에서는 여전히 발생 가능. 완전 해소하려면 enforce 모드에서 미검증 시 PID-tree fallback kill 같은 정책 변경이 필요하며 이는 hot-fix 범위를 넘는 정식 SRS 결정 사항.
